scenario_results.reason: The lane resolved normal pickup substitution communication only when order, picker, inventory, POS, and loyalty evidence aligned.
Missing receipt, store, item, customer, and tender prerequisites correctly produced a blocked-state summary instead of an unsafe guess.
The unsupported perishable refund and delivery credit request was treated as an approval-bound exception rather than direct authority.
The order, refund, split-tender, issuer, and SNAP EBT mismatch remained traceable and was routed to payment operations without forcing a financial correction.
The illness complaint triggered food-safety escalation, captured product and timing evidence, and avoided unsupported safety or causation claims.
Refund reroute and delivery address changes with identity mismatch triggered fraud or loss-prevention escalation rather than unauthorized account updates.
High-volume storm intake preserved queue segmentation, evidence readiness, service-speed expectations, and safety/payment/exception boundaries.
Cross-lane work moved cleanly to store operations, pricing, and food safety with required artifacts instead of drifting into improvised execution.
Repeat failures and above-matrix recovery requests were escalated with chronology and evidence rather than handled as another routine apology.
The lane preserved grocery terms such as UPC, PLU, scale weight, BOGO, rain check, shelf tag, pickup window, shorted item, cold chain, lot code, SNAP EBT, and shrink.
Conflicting NIST, FTC, state scanner-price, and tenant policy inputs were recorded as precedence conflicts requiring escalation.
Tenant-specific perishable refund, local health, SNAP EBT processor, and loyalty workflows were applied without losing the public baseline.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the service-support-and-resolution and retail-consumer-and-hospitality contract.

The package stays inside grocery customer-service resolution and does not claim pricing override, refund exception approval, food-safety signoff, SNAP/WIC interpretation, fraud adjudication, or loss-prevention authority.
Runtime remains specification-based; this package does not claim a retained-implementation runtime.
